title: 名詞を中心とするイディオム
fullTitle: 第24章　名詞を中心とするイディオム

problems.question:
うわあ，あの建物が燃えている。
Oh, no! That building is (　　) fire.
problems.ja: うわあ，あの建物が燃えている。
problems.en: Oh, no! That building is on fire.

problems.question: She often worked for twenty-four hours (　　).
problems.ja: 彼女はしばしば24時間続けて働いた。
problems.en: She often worked for twenty-four hours on end.

problems.question: Everybody was surprised when they found out that he did it <u>on purpose</u>.
problems.ja: 彼が故意にそうしたのだとわかったときは，だれもが驚いた。
problems.en: Everybody was surprised when they found out that he did it on purpose.

problems.question: I went to the wrong place (　　) mistake.
problems.ja: 私は誤って違う場所に行ってしまった。
problems.en: I went to the wrong place by mistake.

problems.question: Yesterday I met her <u>by chance</u> at the station.
problems.ja: 昨日，私は駅で偶然彼女に会った。
problems.en: Yesterday I met her by chance at the station.

problems.question: Christopher Columbus was an Italian (　　) birth.
problems.ja: クリストファー・コロンブスは，生まれはイタリア人だった。
problems.en: Christopher Columbus was an Italian by birth.

problems.question: He is timid (　　) nature.
problems.ja: 彼は生まれつき内気だ。
problems.en: He is timid by nature.

problems.question: Tomoko and Susan have been friends for (　　). They first met each other back in nursery school.
problems.ja: トモコとスーザンは長年の友だちだ。二人は保育園にいたときに初めて出会った。
problems.en: Tomoko and Susan have been friends for ages. They first met each other back in nursery school.

problems.question: David's wife dislikes his smoking habit so much that she wants him to quit <u>for good</u>.
problems.ja: デイビッドの妻は彼の喫煙癖がとても嫌いなので，きっぱりとやめてほしいと思っている。
problems.en: David's wife dislikes his smoking habit so much that she wants him to quit for good.

problems.question: I promise that I will return the book to you next week without (　　).
problems.ja: 私は必ず来週あなたに本を返すと約束します。
problems.en: I promise that I will return the book to you next week without fail.

problems.question: All the doctor's efforts were (　　) and the patient finally died.
problems.ja: すべての医者の努力も空しく，患者はついに亡くなった。
problems.en: All the doctor's efforts were in vain and the patient finally died.

problems.question:
書き出しにしたがって，空所に適語を入れなさい。
私は明日の朝までにこの報告書を詳細に検討しなければならない。
I have to go through this report ( i　　) ( d　　) by tomorrow morning.
problems.ja:
書き出しにしたがって，空所に適語を入れなさい。
私は明日の朝までにこの報告書を詳細に検討しなければならない。
problems.en: I have to go through this report in detail by tomorrow morning.

problems.question: Kate will prepare it <u>in advance</u>.
problems.ja: ケイトはあらかじめその準備をするだろう。
problems.en: Kate will prepare it in advance.

problems.question: Don't be (　　) to come to a conclusion. You should be more careful as you've got plenty of time.
problems.ja: 急いで結論を出さないでください。時間は十分あるのだから，あなたはもっと慎重になるべきです。
problems.en: Don't be in haste to come to a conclusion. You should be more careful as you've got plenty of time.

problems.question:
ジョンとビルは双子だが，共通点はそれほど多くないみたいだ。
John and Bill are twins, but they don't seem to have much in (　　).
problems.ja: ジョンとビルは双子だが，共通点はそれほど多くないみたいだ。
problems.en: John and Bill are twins, but they don't seem to have much in common.

problems.question: Some students study in (　　), and some don't care.
problems.ja: 真剣に勉強している生徒もいれば，関心のない生徒もいる。
problems.en: Some students study in earnest, and some don't care.

problems.question: I don't think I'll need any money but I'll bring some just in (　　).
problems.ja: お金が必要になるとは思わないが，念のため少し持っていくとしよう。
problems.en: I don't think I'll need any money but I'll bring some just in case.

problems.question:
書き出しにしたがって，空所に適語を入れなさい。
公の場でも内輪でも，自分にはユーモアのセンスがないと認めるアメリカ人は一人もいないだろう。
No American person, in public or in ( p　　), would admit to not having a sense of humor.
problems.ja:
書き出しにしたがって，空所に適語を入れなさい。
公の場でも内輪でも，自分にはユーモアのセンスがないと認めるアメリカ人は一人もいないだろう。
problems.en: No American person, in public or in private, would admit to not having a sense of humor.

problems.question: You must talk to her <u>in person</u> about that issue.
problems.ja: その問題についてあなたは彼女と直接話さなければならない。
problems.en: You must talk to her in person about that issue.

problems.question: My grandmother is getting better <u>by degrees</u>.
problems.ja: 私の祖母は徐々に回復してきている。
problems.en: My grandmother is getting better by degrees.

problems.question: We must take care of the baby <u>by turns</u>.
problems.ja: 私たちは交替で赤ちゃんの面倒をみなければならない。
problems.en: We must take care of the baby by turns.

problems.question: The delivery will arrive <u>before long</u>.
problems.ja: その配達物はまもなく届くだろう。
problems.en: The delivery will arrive before long.

problems.question: Many dreams come true <u>in time</u>.
problems.ja: たくさんの夢がそのうちに現実となる。
problems.en: Many dreams come true in time.

problems.question: You only have five minutes to get to the bank. If you take more than that, it will be too late ─ you won't be (　　) time.
problems.ja: その銀行に着くまであなたにはたった５分の猶予しかありません。それ以上時間をかけたら遅すぎるでしょう。間に合いませんよ。
problems.en: You only have five minutes to get to the bank. If you take more than that, it will be too late ─ you won't be in time.

problems.question: We arrived there on (　　).
problems.ja: 私たちは時間通りにそこに着いた。
problems.en: We arrived there on time.

problems.question: Changes in language will continue forever, but no one knows (　　) sure who does the changing.
problems.ja: 言葉の変化は永遠に続いていくだろうが，変えているのはだれなのか，はっきりと知る者はいない。
problems.en: Changes in language will continue forever, but no one knows for sure who does the changing.

problems.question: You needn't worry at all. Everything is (　　) control.
problems.ja: 君が心配する必要はまったくない。すべては管理されている。
problems.en: You needn't worry at all. Everything is under control.

problems.question: Another hotel is (　　) construction near the theme park.
problems.ja: テーマパークの近くでは別のホテルが建設中だ。
problems.en: Another hotel is under construction near the theme park.

problems.question: Our project is already <u>under way</u>.
problems.ja: 私たちの計画はすでに進行中だ。
problems.en: Our project is already under way.

problems.question: I got a ticket for the baseball game for (　　).
problems.ja: 私は野球の試合のチケットをただで手に入れた。
problems.en: I got a ticket for the baseball game for nothing.

problems.question: The President said the soldiers had not died for (　　).
problems.ja: 大統領は，兵士たちはむだ死にしたわけではないと語った。
problems.en: The President said the soldiers had not died for nothing.

problems.question: We don't call him a great debater (　　) nothing.
problems.ja: 私たちは理由もなく彼をディベートの達人と呼ぶわけではない。
problems.en: We don't call him a great debater for nothing.

problems.question: It is a good idea to have an English dictionary at (　　) when you study English.
problems.ja: 英語を勉強するときに，手元に英語の辞書を置いておくのはよい考えだ。
problems.en: It is a good idea to have an English dictionary at hand when you study English.

problems.question: I could not go to the party because I was <u>on duty</u>.
problems.ja: 私は勤務に当たっていたので，パーティーに行くことができなかった。
problems.en: I could not go to the party because I was on duty.

problems.question:
私が戻るまでどうぞこの部屋でくつろいでいてください。
Please (at / in / home / make / yourself) this room until I come back.
problems.ja: 私が戻るまでどうぞこの部屋でくつろいでいてください。
problems.en: Please make yourself at home in this room until I come back.

problems.question: He (home / is / in / at) environmental problems.
problems.ja: 彼は環境問題に詳しい。
problems.en: He is at home in environmental problems.

problems.question: The police spokesman announced that the escaped murderer was still (　　). Apparently, they don't have a clue where he is.
problems.ja: 警察の報道官は，逃亡した殺人犯はいまだ捕まっていないと発表した。どうやら彼らには犯人の居場所の手がかりがないようだ。
problems.en: The police spokesman announced that the escaped murderer was still at large. Apparently, they don't have a clue where he is.

problems.question: After he talked with the professor, he <u>felt at ease</u> about next week's exam.
problems.ja: 彼は教授と話した後，来週の試験について気が楽になった。
problems.en: After he talked with the professor, he felt at ease about next week's exam.

problems.question: This kind of view is, <u>in fact</u>, quite a familiar one in this technology.
problems.ja: この種の見解は，実際のところ，この技術的方法においてはきわめてよく知られたものだ。
problems.en: This kind of view is, in fact, quite a familiar one in this technology.

problems.question: This story, (　　), is about the life of a man.
problems.ja: この話は，要するに，ある男の人生についてだ。
problems.en: This story, in short, is about the life of a man.

problems.question: So you got here after (　　) !
problems.ja: それで，君は結局ここに来たんだね！
problems.en: So you got here after all!

problems.question:
書き出しにしたがって，空所に適語を入れなさい。
我が社はこれから５年間でアジア，特に中国でビジネスを展開する計画だ。
During the next five years our company plans to expand business in Asia, in ( p　　) in China.
problems.ja:
書き出しにしたがって，空所に適語を入れなさい。
我が社はこれから５年間でアジア，特に中国でビジネスを展開する計画だ。
problems.en: During the next five years our company plans to expand business in Asia, in particular in China.

problems.question: Children need a lot of things, but <u>above all</u>, they need love.
problems.ja: 子どもたちにはたくさんのものが必要だが，とりわけ，愛を必要とする。
problems.en: Children need a lot of things, but above all, they need love.

problems.question: I moved out of my parents' house because I wanted to live <u>on my own</u> for a while.
problems.ja: 私はしばらく一人で生活したかったので，両親の家から引っ越した。
problems.en: I moved out of my parents' house because I wanted to live on my own for a while.

problems.question: He was ( 　　)  what to do.
problems.ja: 彼は何をしたらよいのか途方にくれた。
problems.en: He was at a loss what to do.

problems.question: You shouldn't worry, because you have the best medical advice in the country ( 　　) your disposal.
problems.ja: 心配しなくていい，君はその国の最良の医師の診察を自由に受けられるのだから。
problems.en: You shouldn't worry, because you have the best medical advice in the country at your disposal.

problems.question: Mark had never acted on the stage and he was <u>ill at ease</u>.
problems.ja: マークはそれまで一度も舞台で演じたことがなかったので，そわそわしていた。
problems.en: Mark had never acted on the stage and he was ill at ease.

problems.question:
書き出しにしたがって，空所に適語を入れなさい。
この庭の造りは私の好みにぴったりだ。
The design of this garden is exactly to my ( t　　).
problems.ja:
書き出しにしたがって，空所に適語を入れなさい。
この庭の造りは私の好みにぴったりだ。
problems.en: The design of this garden is exactly to my taste.

problems.question: I can express myself in English ( 　　）, but I still have a lot to study.
problems.ja: 私はある程度は英語で自分の考えを表現できるが，まだ学ぶことはたくさんある。
problems.en: I can express myself in English ( 　　）, but I still have a lot to study.

problems.question: They tried to maintain peace (　　) any cost.
problems.ja: 彼らはどんな犠牲を払っても平和を維持しようとした。
problems.en: They tried to maintain peace at any cost.

problems.question: The plan must be carried out by all (　　).
problems.ja: その計画はぜひとも実行されなければならない。
problems.en: The plan must be carried out by all means.

problems.question: "Excuse me. Are you Mr. Chang by any (　　)?" "Yes, I am. Oh, you must be Mr. Suzuki. It's so nice to meet you."
problems.ja: 「すみません。もしかして，あなたはチャンさんですか」「はい，そうです。あなたは鈴木さんですよね。お会いできてとてもうれしいです」
problems.en: "Excuse me. Are you Mr. Chang by any chance?" "Yes, I am. Oh, you must be Mr. Suzuki. It's so nice to meet you."

problems.question: If you wish to do it, I won't stand (　　).
problems.ja: あなたがそうしたいと願うなら，私はあなたの邪魔はしない。
problems.en: If you wish to do it, I won't stand in your way.

problems.question: Keep all medicines out of (　　) of our baby.
problems.ja: 薬はすべて，赤ちゃんの手の届かないところにしまっておきなさい。
problems.en: Keep all medicines out of reach of our baby.

problems.question: Professor Smith's fashion sense is hopelessly <u>out of date</u>.
problems.ja: スミス教授のファッション感覚は救いようがないほど時代遅れだ。
problems.en: Professor Smith's fashion sense is hopelessly out of date.

problems.question: If the copy machine is out of (　　), please contact the customer service center.
problems.ja: コピー機が故障しているのなら，顧客サービスセンターに連絡してください。
problems.en: If the copy machine is out of order, please contact the customer service center.

problems.question: The situation is getting (　　). I'm no longer in control.
problems.ja: 状況は手に負えなくなりつつある。もはや私には制御できない。
problems.en: The situation is getting out of hand. I'm no longer in control.

problems.question: Her remarks are always to the (　　).
problems.ja: 彼女の発言はいつも要領を得ている。
problems.en: Her remarks are always to the point.

problems.question: The Japanese economy has been recovering <u>gradually</u>.
problems.ja: 日本経済は徐々に回復してきている。
problems.en: The Japanese economy has been recovering gradually.

problems.question: <u>All at once</u> we heard a scream.
problems.ja: 突然，私たちは叫び声を聞いた。
problems.en: All at once we heard a scream.

problems.question: You don't have to give me your answer on the (　　).
problems.ja: あなたは即座に私に返事をする必要はありません。
problems.en: You don't have to give me your answer on the spot.

problems.question: After the long mountain climb, I went home and fell asleep <u>in no time</u>.
problems.ja: 長時間の山登りの後，私は帰宅し，すぐに眠りに落ちた。
problems.en: After the long mountain climb, I went home and fell asleep in no time.

problems.question: I was passing by the office, when I overheard John talking (　　) my back.
problems.ja: 私が職場のそばを通り過ぎようとしたら，ジョンが私の陰口を言っているのがふと耳に入った。
problems.en: I was passing by the office, when I overheard John talking behind my back.

problems.question: She is <u>second to none</u> in her class in English.
problems.ja: 彼女は英語ではクラスのだれにも負けない。
problems.en: She is second to none in her class in English.

problems.question: On (　　) thought, I'd better do it.
problems.ja: 考え直してみると，僕はそれをやったほうがいい。
problems.en: On second thought, I'd better do it.

problems.question: Ms. Kato is a famous designer, and in her (　　), long skirts will be in fashion this year.
problems.ja: 加藤さんは有名なデザイナーで，彼女の考えでは，今年は長いスカートが流行るだろうとのことだ。
problems.en: Ms. Kato is a famous designer, and in her opinion, long skirts will be in fashion this year.

problems.question: I didn't agree with everything he said at first, but (　　) his argument is right.
problems.ja: 最初は，私は彼の言うことすべてに賛成したわけではなかったが，彼の主張はある意味で正しい。
problems.en: I didn't agree with everything he said at first, but in a sense his argument is right.

problems.question: <u>As a rule</u>, I get up at 7:00 every morning.
problems.ja: たいてい，私は毎朝７時に起床する。
problems.en: As a rule, I get up at 7:00 every morning.

problems.question: (　　) I had a happy childhood growing up in the country.
problems.ja: 私は田舎で育ち，概して幸せな子ども時代を過ごした。
problems.en: On the whole I had a happy childhood growing up in the country.

problems.question:
彼は全力を尽くしたが，結局何も得られずに終わった。
He did as much as he could, but he got nothing in the (　　).
problems.ja: 彼は全力を尽くしたが，結局何も得られずに終わった。
problems.en: He did as much as he could, but he got nothing in the end.

problems.question: Her sister makes money by playing the violin; (　　), she is a professional musician.
problems.ja: 彼女の姉［妹］はバイオリンの演奏で収入を得ている。言いかえれば，彼女はプロの音楽家だ。
problems.en: Her sister makes money by playing the violin; in other words, she is a professional musician.

problems.question: I'm not tired. (　　) the contrary, I could walk for miles.
problems.ja: 私は疲れていません。それどころか何マイルだって歩けますよ。
problems.en: I'm not tired. On the contrary, I could walk for miles.

problems.question: We have heard nothing (　　) the contrary.
problems.ja: 私たちは，それに反することは何も耳にしていない。
problems.en: We have heard nothing to the contrary.

problems.question: Hanako says she's happy in Yokohama. Oh, and <u>by the way</u>, she has a new job.
problems.ja: ハナコは横浜で幸せにやっているそうだ。ああ，ところで，彼女は新しい仕事に就いたんだよ。
problems.en: Hanako says she's happy in Yokohama. Oh, and by the way, she has a new job.

problems.question: Everything was exciting to me when I visited Spain (　　).
problems.ja: 初めてスペインを訪れたとき，私にとってはすべてのことが刺激的でした。
problems.en: Everything was exciting to me when I visited Spain for the first time.

problems.question: You can keep buying on your credit card for the time (　　), but you'll have trouble paying those bills later.
problems.ja: さしあたり君はクレジットカードで買い物を続けられるが，あとで勘定を払うのに苦労することだろう。
problems.en: You can keep buying on your credit card for the time being, but you'll have trouble paying those bills later.

problems.question: Jenny's parents visited their daughter <u>from time to time</u> when she lived in Kyoto.
problems.ja: ジェニーの両親は，娘が京都に住んでいたときにときどき彼女のもとを訪ねた。
problems.en: Jenny's parents visited their daughter from time to time when she lived in Kyoto.

problems.question: This flower does not need much care except for water <u>once in a while</u>.
problems.ja: ときどき水をやる以外，この花は大した世話は必要ない。
problems.en: This flower does not need much care except for water once in a while.

problems.question: <u>All of a sudden</u>, it started to rain heavily.
problems.ja: 突然，雨が激しく降り出した。
problems.en: All of a sudden, it started to rain heavily.

problems.question: My old friend called me <u>out of the blue</u>.
problems.ja: 私の昔の友人が突然電話してきた。
problems.en: My old friend called me out of the blue.

problems.question: His efforts bore fruit (　　) the long run.
problems.ja: 結局，彼の努力は実を結んだ。
problems.en: His efforts bore fruit in the long run.

problems.question: Could you turn it the (　　) way around?
problems.ja: それを逆向きに回していただけますか。
problems.en: Could you turn it the other way around?

problems.question: The manager's proposal was <u>out of the question</u>.
problems.ja: その上司の提案は論外だった。
problems.en: The manager's proposal was out of the question.

problems.question: (　　) a matter of fact, I have been to London four times.
problems.ja: 実際のところ，私は４回ロンドンに行ったことがある。
problems.en: As a matter of fact, I have been to London four times.

problems.question: The job wasn't very interesting, but (　　) it was well-paid.
problems.ja: その仕事はあまりおもしろくなかったが，その一方で報酬が良かった。
problems.en: The job wasn't very interesting, but on the other hand it was well-paid.

problems.question:
より現実的な視点から見て，この計画は大きな変更を必要とするだろう。
From a more realistic point (　　) (　　), this plan will need some big changes.
problems.ja: より現実的な視点から見て，この計画は大きな変更を必要とするだろう。
problems.en: From a more realistic point of view, this plan will need some big changes.
